snp5.5:1.1:
I ask you, Blessed One.
Please tell me.
snp5.5:1.3: I regard you as an attainer-of-knowledge, developed in mind.
snp5.5:1.4: From what have the many forms of stress
snp5.5:1.5:
arisen in the world?
The Buddha:
snp5.5:2.1: You ask me the source of stress.
snp5.5:2.3: I’ll tell it to you as one who discerns.
snp5.5:2.4: From acquisition as cause the many forms of stress
snp5.5:2.5: come into being in the world.
snp5.5:3.2: —the dullard— comes to stress again & again.
snp5.5:3.3: Therefore, discerning, you shouldn’t create acquisitions
snp5.5:3.4:
as you stay focused on the birth & origin of stress.
Mettagū:
snp5.5:4.2:
Now we ask something else.
Please tell us.
snp5.5:4.3: How do the enlightened cross over the flood of
snp5.5:4.5: Please, sage, declare this to me
snp5.5:4.6:
as this Dhamma has been known by you.
The Buddha:
snp5.5:5.1: I will expound to you Dhamma
snp5.5:5.5:
you’ll cross over beyond entanglement in the world.
Mettagū:
snp5.5:6.4:
I’ll cross over beyond entanglement in the world.
The Buddha:
snp5.5:7.4: Dispelling any delight, any entrenchment in those things,
snp5.5:7.5: consciousness should not take a stance in becoming.
snp5.5:8.1: The monk who dwells thus —mindful, heedful—
snp5.5:8.2: letting go of his sense of mine, knowing right here would abandon
snp5.5:9.1: I relish, Gotama, the Great Seer’s words
snp5.5:9.3: for yes, Blessed One, you’ve abandoned stress
snp5.5:9.4: as this Dhamma has been known by you.
snp5.5:10.1: And they, too, would abandon stress
snp5.5:10.2: those whom you, sage, would admonish unceasingly.
snp5.5:10.3: Having met you, I bow down to you, Nāga.
snp5.5:10.4:
Perhaps you will admonish me unceasingly.
The Buddha:
snp5.5:11.1: Whoever you recognize as a brahman, an attainer-of-knowledge
snp5.5:11.2: possessing nothing, unentangled in sensuality & becoming—
snp5.5:11.3: yes, he has crossed over this flood.
snp5.5:11.4: Having crossed to the far shore, he’s free from rigidity, free from doubt.
snp5.5:12.1: And anyone who has realized, who is an attainer-of-knowledge here,
snp5.5:12.2: having unentangled the bond to becoming and non-,
snp5.5:12.4: I tell you, has crossed over birth & aging.
